title: Cape Verde Draw Saudi Arabia to Miraculously Reach Round of 32

original_text:
Cape Verde Draw Saudi Arabia to Miraculously Reach Round of 32
Key Takeaways
Cape Verde drew 0-0 with Saudi Arabia to finish second in Group H, reaching the World Cup knockout stage on their tournament debut.
The result eliminated Saudi Arabia, who finish bottom of Group H with two points from three matches.
Cape Verde finish the group stage unbeaten with three draws and will face Argentina in the round of 32.
Cape Verde reached the knockout stages with a draw against Saudi Arabia (Photo by Francois Nel/Getty Images)
Cape Verde secured a place in the World Cup knockout stage on their tournament debut after a 0-0 draw with Saudi Arabia in Houston, finishing second in Group H behind Spain and eliminating Saudi Arabia and Uruguay from the competition.
The result was confirmed by events in the group's other fixture, where Spain beat Uruguay 1-0, meaning a single point was enough for Cape Verde to progress.
Cagey First Half Ends Goalless In Houston
Both sides struggled to create clear chances in the opening 45 minutes. Cape Verde's best opportunity came when Willy Semedo forced a low save from goalkeeper Mohammed Al Owais near the near post, while Saudi Arabia's only shot on target arrived in injury time, with Mohamed Kanno heading a Sultan Mandash cross straight at Vozinha.
Saudi Arabia suffered an early setback when defender Hassan Al Tambakti was stretchered off in the 32nd minute and replaced by Ali Lajami. Both sides picked up early yellow cards, with Cape Verde's Wagner Pina booked in the seventh minute and Saudi Arabia's Saud Abdulhamid cautioned in the second minute for a high challenge.
Late Chances Go Unconverted As Stalemate Holds
The second half followed a similar pattern, with neither side able to find a breakthrough despite increased pressure from Cape Verde. Substitute Laros Duarte forced a save from Al Owais moments after entering the match in the 71st minute, and Wagner Pina was denied by a crucial block from Abdulelah Al Amri in the 84th minute after appearing to have an open look at goal.
Saudi Arabia's best late opportunity came from Nasser Al-Dawsari, who was booked in the 66th minute for a foul on substitute Nuno da Costa. In the closing seconds, Cape Verde came closest to snatching a winner when Garry Rodrigues set up Da Costa in front of an open net, but the forward's effort went wide.
Cape Verde Make History As Saudi Arabia Are Eliminated
The draw means Cape Verde finish the group stage unbeaten through three matches, all draws, after also holding Spain and Uruguay level in their opening two games. They will face defending champions Argentina in the round of 32. Saudi Arabia, who needed a win to keep their hopes alive, finish bottom of Group H with two points and are eliminated from the tournament.
